# UC-12.7.10 · Control-M Job Dependency Chain Critical Path Delay Analysis

## Description

Batch chains often have a single pacing job; when its runtime stretches, every downstream consumer misses the file or database refresh window. Aggregating runtime percentiles per order_id highlights the critical path before the last hop fails.

## Value

Protects downstream SLAs for billing, supply chain, and data products fed by Control-M orchestration.

## Implementation

If predecessor metadata is not in events, join to a topology CSV exported from Control-M Planning. Compare each order_id’s p90 runtime to the same folder’s peer median to catch chains that lag their own history without maintaining SLA lookup tables.

## SPL

```spl
index=batch sourcetype="controlm:job"
| eval start_epoch=coalesce(start_epoch, strptime(start_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval end_epoch=coalesce(end_epoch, strptime(end_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval runtime_sec=end_epoch-start_epoch
| where isnotnull(runtime_sec) AND runtime_sec >= 0
| stats median(runtime_sec) as med_rt, perc90(runtime_sec) as p90_rt, values(job_name) as chain, count as runs by order_id, folder
| eventstats median(p90_rt) as folder_p90_peer by folder
| where p90_rt > folder_p90_peer * 1.5 AND runs >= 3
| sort -p90_rt
```

## Visualization

Gantt-style timeline panel, waterfall chart (job runtime by order_id), table of top p90_rt chains.

## References

- [BMC Control-M documentation](https://documents.bmc.com/supportu/controlm/)
